blockers.title: Waitlist promotion can overbook events after capacity edits
blockers.evidence: Spec A2 requires one-tap RSVP with a seat cap plus automatic waitlist. `convex/admin/events.ts:235-240` validates only that capacity is an integer >= 1, and `convex/admin/events.ts:341` patches edited capacity on non-draft events without checking current registered seats. When a registered member cancels, `convex/events.ts:471-484` calls `promoteEarliestWaitlisted`; `convex/events.ts:397-424` promotes the earliest waitlisted row without recomputing registered count against `event.capacity`. Scenario: capacity 10, 10 registered, waitlist exists; admin edits capacity to 5; one cancellation still promotes a waitlisted member even though 9 registered seats remain over the cap.
blockers.required_fix: Reject live capacity edits below the current registered count, or explicitly reconcile excess registrations with audit and notifications. Also make `promoteEarliestWaitlisted` recompute capacity after cancellation and promote only when `registered_count < capacity`. Add regression tests for reduced capacity plus cancellation.

blockers.title: Waitlist promotion bypasses current eligibility and priority-window rules
blockers.evidence: Direct RSVP enforces active lifecycle, lane visibility, and priority-window standing at `convex/events.ts:265-322`. The auto-promotion path does not load the member row or call `laneSeesEvent` or `currentStanding`; it patches any earliest waitlisted registration to `registered` at `convex/events.ts:408-424` and is invoked from cancellation at `convex/events.ts:483-484`. A member can be waitlisted while active, then suspended via `convex/admin/members.ts:531-579`, or a plain Member can remain on the waitlist when a priority window is later opened; the next cancellation gives that account a seat. That violates spec A2, which says during the window only Active Member+ may take a seat, and Stage 0's server-side restriction rule.
blockers.required_fix: Before promotion, load the candidate member and re-check lifecycle, event lane visibility, and priority-window standing. Skip or cancel ineligible waitlist rows with audit and notification. Add tests for suspended waitlisted members and priority-window promotion attempts.

non_blocking_notes: `npm test` could not run in this read-only sandbox because Vitest tried to write under `node_modules/.vite-temp` and hit `EPERM`.
`git diff --check main...HEAD` passed aside from sandbox xcrun cache warnings.
No real secrets found in the diff scan; added member-facing `src`/`convex` strings did not introduce em dashes.

spec_compliance.criterion: A2 events: seat cap, automatic waitlist, and priority window
spec_compliance.evidence: Direct RSVP has the checks, but automatic promotion can overbook and can move currently ineligible members into seats.
Admin edit can lower live capacity below current registered seats with no server-side reconciliation, which then breaks the seat-cap invariant on the next cancellation.
Reviewed apply/list/result paths enforce active lifecycle, lane/audience gates, one application, deadline refusal, evergreen no-application behavior, and winner re-checks.
`convex/directory.ts` lists only active, opted-in, Active Member+ standard/ally rows and omits email, mobile, gender, date of birth, and bio.

spec_compliance.criterion: Brand locks for added member-facing strings
spec_compliance.evidence: No member-facing em dash blocker found; gold usage in the panel diff is confined to recognition surfaces such as the founding-member badge.
Typecheck passed, but full test/build proof was not verified in this read-only Gate 4 run because test tooling attempted filesystem writes.

summary: The slice cannot ship. The event waitlist promotion path is not enforcing the same capacity and eligibility rules as RSVP, so server-side event guarantees are not delivered end-to-end.
